**Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS
CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. ---

**Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics**

**Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?**

Amal Doua  
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester  
31/03/2017

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS
CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

**Breadcrumb:**
HOME / ARCHIVES / VOL. 6 (2017) / Articles

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS
CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

**Breadcrumb:**
HOME / ARCHIVES / VOL. 6 (2017) / Articles

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Header:**

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS
CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

HOME / ARCHIVES / VOL. 6 (2017) / Articles

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS
CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

HOME / ARCHIVES / VOL. 6 (2017) / Articles

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Amal Doua

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics**

**Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?**

Amal Doua  
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester  
31/03/2017

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.  

On the third day, Hiccup brings a sack of fish, and mentions that it contains Icelandic cod and salmon. There is also a pond in the clearing, and one scene shows Toothless hunting for fish. However, in this paper, it is assumed that Toothless relies solely on Hiccup for nutrition. During his time in The Cove, it is assumed that his diet consists of only salmon and

**Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS
CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.  

On the third day, Hiccup brings a sack of fish, and mentions that it contains Icelandic cod and salmon. There is also a pond in the clearing, and one scene shows Toothless hunting for fish. ---

**Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics**

**Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?**

Amal Doua  
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester  
31/03/2017

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.  

On the third day, Hiccup brings a sack of fish, and mentions that it contains Icelandic cod and salmon. There is also a pond in the clearing, and one scene shows Toothless hunting for fish. However, in this paper, it is assumed that Toothless relies solely on Hiccup for nutrition. During his time in The Cove, it is assumed that his diet consists of only salmon and

**Header:**

CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Amal Doua

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.

**Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS
CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

**Breadcrumb:**
HOME / ARCHIVES / VOL. 6 (2017) / Articles

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics**

**Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?**

Amal Doua  
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester  
31/03/2017

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.  

On the third day, Hiccup brings a sack of fish, and mentions that it contains Icelandic cod and salmon. There is also a pond in the clearing, and one scene shows Toothless hunting for fish. However, in this paper, it is assumed that Toothless relies solely on Hiccup for nutrition. During his time in The Cove, it is assumed that his diet consists of only salmon and

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics**

**Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?**

Amal Doua  
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester  
31/03/2017

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.  

On the third day, Hiccup brings a sack of fish, and mentions that it contains Icelandic cod and salmon. There is also a pond in the clearing, and one scene shows Toothless hunting for fish. However, in this paper, it is assumed that Toothless relies solely on Hiccup for nutrition. During his time in The Cove, it is assumed that his diet consists of only salmon and

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. ---

**Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics**

**Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?**

Amal Doua  
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester  
31/03/2017

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.  

On the third day, Hiccup brings a sack of fish, and mentions that it contains Icelandic cod and salmon. There is also a pond in the clearing, and one scene shows Toothless hunting for fish. However, in this paper, it is assumed that Toothless relies solely on Hiccup for nutrition. During his time in The Cove, it is assumed that his diet consists of only salmon and

***Abstract***

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics**

**Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?**

Amal Doua  
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester  
31/03/2017

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.  

On the third day, Hiccup brings a sack of fish, and mentions that it contains Icelandic cod and salmon. There is also a pond in the clearing, and one scene shows Toothless hunting for fish. **Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS

HOME / ARCHIVES / VOL. 6 (2017) / Articles

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics**

**Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?**

Amal Doua  
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester  
31/03/2017

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.  

On the third day, Hiccup brings a sack of fish, and mentions that it contains Icelandic cod and salmon. There is also a pond in the clearing, and one scene shows Toothless hunting for fish. **Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS
CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics**

**Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?**

Amal Doua  
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester  
31/03/2017

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.  

On the third day, Hiccup brings a sack of fish, and mentions that it contains Icelandic cod and salmon. There is also a pond in the clearing, and one scene shows Toothless hunting for fish. However, in this paper, it is assumed that Toothless relies solely on Hiccup for nutrition. During his time in The Cove, it is assumed that his diet consists of only salmon and

**Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS
CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

HOME / ARCHIVES / VOL. 6 (2017) / Articles

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

HOME / ARCHIVES / VOL. 6 (2017) / Articles

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. ***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.  

On the third day, Hiccup brings a sack of fish, and mentions that it contains Icelandic cod and salmon. There is also a pond in the clearing, and one scene shows Toothless hunting for fish. The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Amal Doua

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

**Journal of Interdisciplinary Science Topics**

**Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?**

Amal Doua  
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. ***Introduction***  
The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. The events take place in Viking Scotland, in the small village of Berk which is repeatedly attacked by dragons. One of the deadliest dragon species is the Night Fury, which was previously unseen by any living person. It is described as the "unholy offspring of lightning and death itself” due to its speed and camouflage abilities [2]. Toothless is the only known Night Fury, and is shown to be intelligent, curious, and loyal. 

Toothless is in The Cove for a total of twelve days [5]. It is assumed that Hiccup visited once a day for the duration of Toothless’ stay.

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Amal Doua

***Abstract***  
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the *How to Train Your Dragon* (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing a total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

The *How to Train Your Dragon* franchise is loosely based on the popular book series by Cressida Cowell [1]. **Header:**
JOURNAL OF INTERDISCIPLINARY SCIENCE TOPICS
CURRENT ARCHIVES ABOUT ▼ SEARCH Login

**Title:**
Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon’s Diet?

**Author:**
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

**Keywords:**
Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

**PDF Button:**
PDF

**Abstract:**
ABSTRACT
Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon’s diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.

Can Hiccup Supply Enough Fish to Maintain a Dragon's Diet?
Amal Doua
The Centre for Interdisciplinary Science, University of Leicester

Keywords: Film, Biology, Nutrition, Dragon, Toothless, How to Train Your Dragon

ABSTRACT

Toothless is a Night Fury dragon who appears in the How to Train Your Dragon (HTTYD) franchise, and is said to be the rarest, fastest, and most intelligent of the dragon species. In the 2010 film, Toothless is struck down during a dragon raid and loses his left tail-fin, leaving him trapped in a sunken area. This paper investigates the feasibility of Hiccup, a teenage boy, being able to supply enough fish to maintain the dragon's diet by catching and bringing fish to him. It was found that in the twelve days that Toothless was trapped, he would have required 13 Icelandic cod and 56 Atlantic salmon every day, which totalled 716 kg. Hiccup would have had to carry 828 fish weighing at total of 8592 kg over the twelve days, which is unrealistic since he struggles to lift a shield in combat.
